stakes.narrative: A process line must set a single input level per incoming lot; lots arrive UNLABELED with grade compositions that vary lot to lot and may lie outside anything on record; the only per-lot evidence is a small calibration sample of sensor readings. A poor outcome (quality below the scrap line) is far costlier than a forgone gain, so the failing tail under a deliberately set input level is what must be priced -- per lot, from its window.

functionals.name: exceedance
functionals.brief_clause: A poor outcome -- a quality score below the scrap line of -5 -- is far costlier than a forgone gain

n_cal.description: size of the lot's calibration sample; each evaluation regime delivers context.cal_window with that many UNLABELED sensor readings from the target lot (variable length)

control_surface.experiment_note: each experiment call draws ONE fresh lot (hidden, differing composition) and applies your input level to n units from it; several lots require several calls
control_surface.deliverable_note: model(regime, n, seed) receives regime.context['cal_window']: unlabeled sensor readings from the target lot, variable length; your model answers for THAT lot.
